db_connection: N/A — derived from Phase 1 code review (entity list + cross-reference of CatalogContext ef_entities vs. full entity source list).

representations.role: CANONICAL — source of truth for identity, login, email, roles.

CONFIRMED DEAD/ASPIRATIONAL CODE — not registered as a DbSet on CatalogContext (only 7 DbSets: Baskets, CatalogItems, CatalogBrands, CatalogTypes, Orders, OrderItems, BasketItems), not configured via AppIdentityDbContext.OnModelCreating (which only calls base), and no `IRepository<Buyer>`/`_buyerRepository` usage was found anywhere in src/. Buyer and PaymentMethod are unused classes.
representations.action: No further action needed — confirmed not persisted. If Buyer/PaymentMethod functionality is desired, it requires new EF configuration + repository wiring from scratch, not a fix to existing mappings.

representations.role: SHADOW / loose reference — a string foreign key into AspNetUsers.Id, duplicated across two tables, with no enforced FK. Classic 'loose identifier' pattern called out in the skill's canonical/shadow guidance.

findings.recommendation: If Buyer is confirmed unused (dead code), BuyerId string fields are the de-facto canonical link and should be documented as such with an explicit note that referential integrity is app-enforced only. If Buyer IS used, clarify the relationship between Buyer.Id and AspNetUsers.Id / BuyerId strings — there may be three overlapping identifiers for one customer.

representations.role: CANONICAL — live product data, source of truth for price/stock/description.
INTENTIONAL DUPLICATE / historical snapshot — not a shadow entity in the negative sense. Captures product name/price/image at time of order for audit/history purposes.
POSSIBLE SHADOW / DTO — a struct co-located with CatalogItem class. Likely a read-model/value-object for API responses (e.g. combining CatalogItem with brand/type names), not a persisted duplicate.
representations.action: Confirm CatalogItemDetails is used only as a projection/DTO and never independently persisted.

findings.recommendation: No corrective action needed for CatalogItemOrdered — this is good practice (event-sourcing-lite for orders). CatalogItemDetails should be confirmed as a non-persisted projection.

summary: One canonical/shadow check completed per concept area (Customer/Buyer and Catalog Product), as required. The Buyer/PaymentMethod entity mapping gap previously flagged as an open item has now been resolved: confirmed dead/aspirational code, not persisted (see RC-002 in schema-catalogue.json).

change_records.what: Buyer reclassified from 'POSSIBLE SHADOW' (confidence 0.6) to 'CONFIRMED DEAD/ASPIRATIONAL CODE' (confidence 0.9). The de-facto canonical link for customer identity in CatalogDb is Basket.BuyerId / Order.BuyerId (string), unenforced soft references to AspNetUsers.Id.
